input.background: Calculate the variance and standard deviation of the following sample: $2,3,3,3,4$.
input.question: What are the deviations $(x-\bar{x})$ and squared deviations $(x-\bar{x})^2$ for each value in the sample?

output.answer:
The deviations and squared deviations are shown in the table below:

\begin{tabular}{ccc}
\hline$x$ & $(x-\bar{x})$ & $(x-\bar{x})^2$ \\
\hline 2 & -1 & 1 \\
3 & 0 & 0 \\
3 & 0 & 0 \\
3 & 0 & 0 \\
4 & 1 & 1 \\
\hline$\Sigma x=15$ & & $\Sigma(x-\bar{x})^2=2$ \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

meta_info.caption: Computing Measures of Variation
meta_info.chapter: Methods for Describing Sets of Data
